steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $x^{3}+14x^{2}-7x+54$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute the 6
steps.content:
Multiply 6 by each term inside the second parentheses:
$$6(x^2+9) = 6x^2 + 54$$

steps.title: Remove parentheses and group like terms
steps.content:
Rewrite the full expression:
$$x^3 + 8x^2 - 7x + 6x^2 + 54$$
Collect the $x^2$ terms: $8x^2 + 6x^2 = 14x^2$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $x^{3}+14x^{2}-7x+54$: Distributing 6 gives $6x^2+54$; combining $8x^2+6x^2=14x^2$ produces this expression. ✓
  • Choice B — $x^{3}+8x^{2}-7x+54$: Keeps $8x^2$ unchanged, forgetting to add the $6x^2$ from distributing. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{3}+6x^{2}-7x+54$: Replaces $8x^2$ with $6x^2$ instead of adding them. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{3}+9x^{2}-7x+54$: $9x^2$ cannot result from any combination of the terms present. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
